feedback.text: Dear EU, Please note that many companies, publishers and game developers do not regard the video games sold as the property of the buyer. They can be completely arbitrarily destroyed, without prior notice and without any consultation, and the right of users to use the purchased product can be restored. This is a very devastating and dark future. For what I pay, I would like to own it. The idea is surprised if not only game publishers, but also other companies, product groups, rather than owning them, introduce other opcicos. I will not have the truck you bought, the house you bought is not my ulajndon, the book I bought, and the publisher asks him to destroy it because his political and financial interests require it. This is now happening in the video game industry. I would ask you to oppose this draft law so that the video games purchased, the purchaser and the purchaser alone are owned and cannot subsequently be destroyed. Faithfully,
